quotes.text: What Moroni first recorded in the book carrying his own name were vignettes—a brief catalog, if you will—of things he felt needed to be recorded before he died and the Book of Mormon saga ended. These included the words of Christ to his twelve disciples when they were commissioned to bestow the Holy Ghost by the laying on of hands, the prayer by which priests and teachers were ordained, the sacramental prayers, and instructions as to how those who were baptized were to be received into the ‘church of Christ’ and numbered among the ‘people of Christ.
quotes.speaker: Jeffrey R. Holland
quotes.attr: Jeffrey R. Holland, Christ And The New Covenant, p. 332-333

notes: Holy Ghost In Greek holy is hagios most holy. It also is the same term that refers to members of the church (see Romans 1:7; 12:13). Ghost in Greek is pneuma which is the same word as the spirit that is inside of each of us (see Matthew 27:50; 1 Cor. 6:20). The Hebrew equivalent is Holy Spirit (see Psalms 51:11; Isaiah 63:11). Holy is qodesh apartness, holiness, sacredness, separateness; from the root vdq qadash meaning to consecrate, sanctify. Spirit in Hebrew is ruwach meaning the same as the Greek term for ghost.

apostles (Grk. apostolos) a delegate, messenger, one sent forth with orders. From the root apostello to order (one) to go to a place appointed. It comes from two words apw apo of separation and stello to set, place, set in order, arrange.
hands (Heb. yad) hands; strength, power (fig.); sign, monument
lay (Heb. camak) to lean, lay, rest, support, put, uphold

quotes.text: While in every instance the Nephite twelve are spoken of as disciples, the fact remains that they had been endowed with divine authority to be special witnesses for Christ among their own people. Therefore, they were virtually apostles to the Nephite race, although their jurisdiction was, as revealed to Nephi, eventually to be subject to the authority and jurisdiction of Peter and the twelve chosen in Palestine (see Mormon 3:18-19). According to the definition prevailing in the world an apostle is a witness for Christ, or one who evangelizes a certain nation or people. ‘A zealous advocate of a doctrine or cause.’ Therefore the Nephite twelve became apostles, as special witnesses, just as did Joseph Smith and Oliver Cowdery in the Dispensation of the Fulness of Times.
quotes.speaker: Joseph Fielding Smith
quotes.attr: Joseph Fielding Smith, Answer to Gospel Questions, 1:122

quotes.text: Ye shall call on the Father in my name, in mighty prayer; and after ye have done this ye shall have power] Priesthood power is governed by principles of faith and righteousness (see D&C 121:36). In order to properly represent the Lord in any priesthood capacity, the priesthood holder must be a pure and worthy receptacle of the Spirit of the Lord. It is only through personal preparation by worthy living and seeking the guidance of the Spirit in mighty prayer that a priesthood holder will be empowered with divine utterance in speaking for and in behalf of the Lord in all priesthood blessings and ordinances.
quotes.source: Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon
quotes.attr: Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L. Millet, Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon, 4 vols., 4:, p.323
